description:
You are a data-analytics engineer who has been asked to prepare a quick
    performance benchmark of some one-quarter sales CSV files that the marketing
    department placed in  

        /home/user/datasets/performance/  

    1. Source data  
       There are exactly three CSV files in that directory when the task starts.  
         • sales_q1.csv  
         • sales_q2.csv  
         • sales_q3.csv  

       Every file is already in RFC 4180 compliant comma-separated format and
       uses the same header line:

           OrderID,Units,Revenue

       The files contain *only* the order rows shown in the header; no blank
       lines or trailing commas.  (The ground-truth content itself is hidden
       from you, but you may inspect the files in the terminal while you work.)

    2. What you must create  
       A new directory must be created:

           /home/user/analysis_results/

       Inside that directory you must end up with **exactly three** new files:

       2.1 /home/user/analysis_results/all_sales.csv  
           • It must consist of the single header line
                 OrderID,Units,Revenue
             followed by all *nine* data rows from the three source files,
             in the order   q1 rows → q2 rows → q3 rows.  
           • There must be only one header line in the final file (i.e.
             duplicate headers from the second and third quarter files must be
             removed).

       2.2 /home/user/analysis_results/summary.csv  
           • CSV with two columns, Metric and Value, **exactly three lines** in
             total: one header row plus two data rows.  
           • The file must appear in *this* exact form (including capitalisation):

                 Metric,Value
                 Total_Revenue,1650
                 Average_Units_Sold,3.67

             where  
               – Total_Revenue is the sum of the Revenue column across the
                 nine combined rows;  
               – Average_Units_Sold is the arithmetic mean of the Units column,
                 rounded and written with exactly two digits after the decimal
                 point.

       2.3 /home/user/analysis_results/benchmark.log  
           • Plain-text log, **exactly four lines**, one per benchmarked step,
             following this rigid pattern (no extra spaces):  

                 STEP:STEP1_CONCAT|SECONDS:<float>
                 STEP:STEP2_CLEAN_HEADERS|SECONDS:<float>
                 STEP:STEP3_CALC_REVENUE|SECONDS:<float>
                 STEP:STEP4_CALC_AVG_UNITS|SECONDS:<float>

             where <float> is the wall-clock duration of the corresponding
             step in seconds as reported by the GNU `/usr/bin/time -f "%e"`
             utility.  Any valid decimal representation (e.g. 0.002 or 1.34)
             is acceptable.

    3. What defines a “step” for timing purposes  
         STEP1_CONCAT         — physically concatenating the three CSV files  
         STEP2_CLEAN_HEADERS  — removing the duplicate header lines  
         STEP3_CALC_REVENUE   — computing the total revenue figure  
         STEP4_CALC_AVG_UNITS — computing the average units figure  

       Timing must start **immediately** before the command(s) that perform the
       step and stop when the step’s result is fully written to disk.

    4. Additional requirements  
       • All processing must be done with standard, generally available
         GNU/Linux CLI utilities (`cat`, `awk`, `sed`, `csvtool`, etc.).  
       • You do *not* have root privileges.  Do not place files outside
         /home/user.  
       • The three required output files must be the only new files in
         /home/user/analysis_results/.  
       • No extra lines, BOM markers, or hidden characters are allowed in any
         output file—automated tests will compare them byte-for-byte.

    5. Completion criteria checked by the automated grader  
         a) /home/user/analysis_results/ exists.  
         b) all_sales.csv has 10 lines (1 header + 9 data) with the header
            appearing only once.  
         c) summary.csv is byte-identical to the exact 3-line text above.  
         d) benchmark.log exists, has exactly 4 lines, each
            matching the regex  
              ^STEP:STEP[1-4]_[A-Z_]+\\|SECONDS:[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$  
         e) No extra files are present in /home/user/analysis_results/.

    Work in the terminal, verify your own results and timings, and make sure
    the output files satisfy every formatting rule before you finish.
truth:
<!-- 1. Files that exist before the task starts -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/performance/sales_q1.csv"><![CDATA[
OrderID,Units,Revenue
1001,5,250
1002,3,150
1003,2,100
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/datasets/performance/sales_q2.csv"><![CDATA[
OrderID,Units,Revenue
2001,4,200
2002,6,300
2003,1,50
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/datasets/performance/sales_q3.csv"><![CDATA[
OrderID,Units,Revenue
3001,7,350
3002,2,100
3003,3,150
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- 2. Files that must exist after successful task completion -->
    <expected_outputs>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis_results/all_sales.csv"><![CDATA[
OrderID,Units,Revenue
1001,5,250
1002,3,150
1003,2,100
2001,4,200
2002,6,300
2003,1,50
3001,7,350
3002,2,100
3003,3,150
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/analysis_results/summary.csv"><![CDATA[
Metric,Value
Total_Revenue,1650
Average_Units_Sold,3.67
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/analysis_results/benchmark.log" format="regex">
            ^STEP:STEP1_CONCAT\|SECONDS:[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\n
            STEP:STEP2_CLEAN_HEADERS\|SECONDS:[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\n
            STEP:STEP3_CALC_REVENUE\|SECONDS:[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\n
            STEP:STEP4_CALC_AVG_UNITS\|SECONDS:[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\n$
        </file>
    </expected_outputs>
